factors.name: CPI Release Imminent (Wednesday AM)
factors.summary: US May CPI due Wednesday morning with consensus expecting 4.2% annual rate; any upside surprise would trigger Fed repricing and rate-hike expectations, compressing long-duration tech multiples. Bond traders are already positioning for multiple hikes as early as September per Bloomberg.

factors.name: Japan Inflation Beat — BoJ Hike Signal
factors.summary: Japan May PPI came in at 6.3% y/y (vs 5.5% expected) and 0.9% m/m (vs 0.5% expected); Daiwa sees BoJ June hike as move to avoid 'falling behind curve,' signaling global central bank tightening bias and potential USD strength that pressures NQ components with international revenue.

factors.name: Middle East Escalation — US-Iran Strikes
factors.summary: US launched fresh airstrikes on Iran targets (air defense/radar near Hormuz) after Apache helicopter downed; Iran's Foreign Minister Araghchi vowed direct retaliation. Strait of Hormuz closure risk elevated, crude oil spiked intraday but fell $3.10 by close; risk-off sentiment pressures risk assets and extends tech selloff.

factors.name: Tech Sector Momentum Reversal
factors.summary: Jim Cramer noted tech stocks losing key rally qualities; AAPL slid on weak Siri AI reveal, SMCI plunged on $7B equity raise dilution despite AI backlog strength. XLK/QQQ underperforming amid SpaceX IPO jitters and rotation away from mega-cap AI beneficiaries.

factors.name: Real Yields Rising (Inflation Surprise Risk)
factors.summary: Bond traders positioning for faster Fed hikes (September or sooner); nominal yields rising into CPI print Wednesday. Long-duration NDX components (NVDA, MSFT, GOOGL, TSLA, META) highly sensitive to real rate increases, with 100+ multiple stocks facing compression if inflation surprises hot.

factors.name: Dollar Strength (DXY near 99.90, inverse NQ)
factors.summary: USD/JPY elevated, risk-off flows boosting greenback; strong USD headwind for NQ's international-revenue mega-caps (MSFT, GOOGL, AAPL all >40% FX-exposed). Energy Secretary Wright noted Hormuz traffic rising, dampening oil shock fears temporarily but not reversing EM currency pressures.

factors.name: VIX / Risk-Off Mode
factors.summary: Gold tumbled 1.93% below $4,250 on Middle East escalation and higher rate expectations; silver down 3.5% to 11-week low. Risk-off mood and de-risking flows into CPI are headwinds for beta-heavy tech growth names.

factors.name: AI Capex Concerns (SuperMicro Equity Raise)
factors.summary: SMCI announced $7B equity raise despite booming backlog, signaling heavy cash burn and dilution. Market interprets as sign of capex saturation risk; Micron/chip equipment seeing 'supercycle' but visibility into supply constraints is raising valuation concerns.

factors.name: SpaceX IPO Angst & Tech Positioning
factors.summary: SpaceX IPO nearly 4x oversubscribed but retail allocation uncertainty causing jitters; investors retreating from hot AI/growth plays ahead of mega-IPO. Tech sector rotation into value/defensive posture.

factors.name: 24h Price Action: -1.08% Intraday Decline
factors.summary: NAS100 down 1.08% over 24h despite +1.31% 4h bounce; suggests weak support and vulnerable bounce. High intraday ranges (Nasdaq 1,279 pt swing yesterday) indicate shallow conviction for any direction ahead of CPI.

screening.narrative: NAS100 is in Phase 2 of a downside move driven by confluence of real-rate repricing (Japan inflation beat, Fed hike expectations, CPI print looming Wednesday AM) and Middle East geopolitical risk (US-Iran escalation, Hormuz closure concern, risk-off flows). Long-duration mega-cap tech is the most vulnerable to rising real yields, and AI capex euphoria (SuperMicro dilution, SpaceX IPO jitters) is cracking. The index bounced +1.31% in 4h but remains underwater 1.08% on the day, suggesting the bounce is a bear-trap retest rather than a reversal.
catalysts_ahead: US May CPI release Wednesday 08:30 ET (consensus 4.2% y/y; hot print = rate-hike repricing, bearish NQ multiples)
Fed bank stress test results Wednesday 16:00 ET (June 24)
FOMC decision timing (rate-hike forward guidance if imminent)
Iran retaliation threat (further Hormuz risk escalation)
NVDA, MSFT, AAPL earnings (guidance on cloud capex & AI demand sustainability)
Tech sector rotation / QQQ ETF flows into CPI
screening.risk_warning: If CPI comes in COOL (sub-4.0%), real yields fall sharply, and Fed hike bets unwind, NAS100 could rip higher 2–3% as growth multiples expand. Similarly, any Iran-US de-escalation deal or Hormuz reopening would flip risk sentiment bullish and reverse this sell bias. Momentum quant/CTA long positions may also cushion downside if support at 28800 breaks, forcing short covering.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in Phase 2 — CPI print is 16h away; do NOT chase shorts into CPI, as volatility will spike and whipsaws are likely. Better entry: wait for post-CPI reaction to establish direction, then enter on retest of intraday highs (~29200) if bias remains SELL, or short any hot-CPI rip above 29400. Current zone (29050–29150) is chop; avoid.
